title: Construct binary tree from Inorder and Postorder

content.problemStatement:
You are given two integer arrays, **`inorder`** and **`postorder`**, representing the inorder and postorder traversal sequences of a **binary tree**.
Your task is to **reconstruct the original binary tree** from these traversals and return its root.

Function Declaration

**Function Name**



buildTreebuildTreebuildTree



**Parameters**



inorderinorderinorder: A vector of integers representing the inorder traversal of the binary tree.
postorderpostorderpostorder: A vector of integers representing the postorder traversal of the binary tree.



**Return Value**



Returns a pointer to the root node of the reconstructed binary tree.
constraints: All elements in inorder\texttt{inorder}inorder and postorder\texttt{postorder}postorder are unique.\textbf{unique}.unique.
Each element of postorder\texttt{postorder}postorder appears in inorder.\texttt{inorder}.inorder.
The given arrays are valid traversals\textbf{valid traversals}valid traversals of the same binary tree.
The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of **three lines of input**:
The first line contains a single integer NNN — the number of nodes in the binary tree.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the **inorder traversal** of the tree.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the **postorder traversal** of the tree.
For each test case, output on a new line the **Pre-Order Traversal** of the reconstructed binary tree.
Print the node values separated by spaces.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The binary tree constructed from the given traversals is:
        1
       / \
      2   3
     / \ / \
    4  5 6  7

Explanation:
The binary tree looks like:
    1
   /
  2
